name: Fathom
niche: Investigative true-story documentaries: manhunts, heists, hacks and intelligence operations, reconstructed with maps, documents and archival evidence
audience: Viewers who want long-form armchair documentaries with journalistic rigor; fans of investigative internet-mystery storytelling
brief: Drop into a live moment with a timestamp and a place, then zoom out to the stakes. Present tense. Reconstruct events with maps, documents and named actors. Hedge honestly where evidence is thin. End on a thought, never a summary.
topic_bank: The insider who stole 80 million from a casino with a fish tank thermometer
How a teenager grounded 1,500 flights with a phone call
The hunt for the hacker who stole half of Twitter
How one analyst found a spy ring in plain sight
The heist crew that tunneled into a bank vault over a holiday weekend

defaults.voice_instruct: Calm, precise male documentary narrator, mid-low register, measured newsreader trust. Present tense storytelling with real pauses: a beat after each reveal, a longer beat before chapter turns. Slightly tighter pace during escalation, settling quiet and thoughtful at the end with falling cadence. Dry humor read completely straight. Quoted dialogue gets a subtle cadence shift, never a character voice.
defaults.music_vibe: somber investigative ambient, quiet pulse, restrained tension, minimal piano and low strings
defaults.style_suffix: 3d mannequin documentary, faceless matte humanoid mannequin figures with no facial features, stylized 3d reconstruction environment, dark low-key lighting, deep shadows, one red rim-light accent, desaturated cinematic documentary grade, filmic grain
defaults.negative_style: cartoon, anime, illustration, flat drawing, detailed human faces, facial features, oversaturated, neon colors, bright cheerful lighting, watermark, readable text, gibberish letters, low quality, deformed
